meta.title: depa Smart City Leadership
meta.description: Smart City Leadership by depa: official cohort details, alumni directory, and a multilingual archive for Thailand's smart-city executive program.

text.open: Cohort {{cohort}} is accepting applications through {{deadline}} on depa's official announcement page.
text.closed: Cohort {{cohort}} applications have closed. Review the official depa page for the course structure and source details.
text.inSession: Cohort {{cohort}} is currently underway. The official depa page remains the source of record for the program details.
text.completed: Cohort {{cohort}} has concluded. The official depa page remains the source of record for the program overview.

hero.title: Smart City Leadership
hero.valueStatement: A serious short-format program for leaders who need sharper judgment about how smart-city work actually gets delivered.
hero.subtitle: SCL is most useful when read not as a promotional course page, but as a compact operating brief: it helps public and private leaders understand the strategic, institutional, and implementation logic behind credible smart-city projects in Thailand.
hero.imageCaption: The strongest value is what happens when the cohort can step into real environments together and compare how systems actually operate.

status.inSession: The current cohort is now underway
status.completed: The latest published cohort has concluded

facts.modelValue: Classroom instruction, domestic study visits, and {{speakers}}+ speakers

judgment.title: It compresses strategic judgment.
judgment.description: The value is not just exposure to smart-city terminology, but a clearer sense of what to prioritize, what to question, and what a serious city program should look like.

implementation.title: It is grounded in delivery, not slogans.
implementation.description: The official structure combines expert teaching with institutional references and study visits, which keeps the conversation close to operating reality.

scope.title: It gives leaders a coherent lens across domains.
scope.description: Environment, mobility, energy, governance, people, economy, and livability are treated as part of one city system rather than isolated themes.

investment.title: It signals a meaningful commitment.
investment.description: The time and fee structure place the program closer to an executive commitment than a casual seminar, which changes the level of discussion inside the cohort.

about.title: A compact course for leaders who need implementation clarity
about.description: The official depa outline positions SCL as a fast executive program covering smart-city project design, digital adoption across public and private contexts, and the practical coordination required to move city initiatives forward.

strategy.title: Sharper strategic framing
strategy.description: A structured view of what makes a smart-city initiative credible, livable, and aligned with city priorities.

delivery.title: Practical delivery references
delivery.description: Examples of how digital systems, operations, and civic infrastructure are applied in real organizations.

network.title: A stronger leadership network
network.description: A cohort environment that connects government, academia, utilities, and private-sector operators.

about.learningSitesTitle: Learning references named in the official article
about.learningSitesDescription: The published SCL #6 announcement highlights site-based learning and organizational references including public infrastructure, digital operations, creative economy institutions, and immersive technology labs.
about.imageCaption: The program is strongest when it reads as a working conversation among leaders, not as a promotional event.

pillarStats.environment: Active in 32 of 37 smart city zones
pillarStats.economy: THB 11.9B+ invested through smart city programs
pillarStats.energy: EGAT + SWOC among SCL study-visit partners

journey.title: Structured like an executive sprint
journey.subtitle: SCL combines classroom instruction, curated site references, and a cohort format that helps participants translate policy and technology themes into an implementation lens.

classroom.title: Focused classroom sessions
classroom.description: Core instruction is delivered in a compact format for leaders who need signal, not academic sprawl.

field.title: Domestic study visits
field.description: Participants examine how digital systems and operating models are applied in real Thai organizations.

network.title: Cross-sector cohort exchange
network.description: The cohort mixes public and private participants, which turns the program into a stronger coordination space.

schedule.title: Current published logistics
schedule.description: These details follow depa's official SCL #6 article as of March 7, 2026.
schedule.venueValue: The Quarter Ladprao by UHG, Bangkok, plus domestic study visits

process.title: Application path
process.description: The official article states that applicants should review the course details, download the form from depa, and submit their materials by email.

review.title: Review the official depa announcement
review.description: Use the source page to confirm the scope, schedule, and whether the program fits your role.

submit.title: Download and email the application
submit.description: The article directs applicants to download the form and submit it through the listed email channels.

attend.title: Join the seven-day cohort
attend.description: Accepted participants move into the short-format executive program and domestic study visits.

fieldwork.title: The real differentiator is the access that keeps opening after the cohort
fieldwork.description: SCL is strongest when the cohort can reassemble around live systems. The network periodically returns to operating environments, technical briefings, and site study sessions so members continue to read smart-city work through implementation, not slogans.

cadence.title: Periodic reconvenings
cadence.description: The network can regroup a few times each year for targeted visits, technical walkthroughs, and focused updates.

access.title: High-value sites
access.description: The draw is access to meaningful places such as EEC environments, industrial solution factories, labs, and operating centers.

context.title: Interpretation with peers
context.description: These visits matter because participants read them with other leaders who understand regulation, procurement, infrastructure, and execution.

journey.sourceNote: Application timing, fee, venue, and schedule are based on depa's official SCL #6 article.

alumni.title: Executive Alumni
alumni.subtitle: Search across {{count}} leaders from {{cohorts}} official SCL cohorts, with resilient matching for Thai and English names pulled from depa announcement lists.

network.title: The alumni value is not just the directory. It is the continuing access.
network.description: Beyond the course itself, the SCL network periodically reconvenes around study visits and closed-door learning sessions. Members are occasionally brought into smart-city field visits in Bangkok and elsewhere, including high-value sites such as the Eastern Economic Corridor, solution factories, control rooms, and applied technology labs.
network.caption: SCL alumni activity is designed to keep leaders close to real operating environments, not only classroom ideas.

visits.title: Recurring study visits
visits.description: A few times each year, the network can reopen around scientific and technical site visits that extend the learning beyond the original cohort.

access.title: Access to meaningful sites
access.description: The draw is the quality of access: not tourist stops, but factories, labs, city systems, and development zones where real smart-city solutions are being built.

peers.title: A stronger SCL family
peers.description: These trips work because the alumni base already includes public agencies, utilities, universities, and private operators who can interpret what they are seeing together.

alumni.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, role, organization, or cohort...
alumni.searchHint: Search is normalized from the official announcement text so OCR spacing issues do not block matches.
alumni.positionDisclaimer: Positions and affiliations shown reflect the time each participant joined the program. Titles and organizations may have changed since.
alumni.noResults: No alumni found matching your search.

gallery.title: Inside the Experience
gallery.subtitle: A visual archive from workshops, site visits, and cohort sessions collected in the SCL media set.

footer.description: A multilingual landing page for depa Smart City Leadership, combining verified cohort details, alumni search, and a visual archive in one place.
footer.sourceNote: This landing page links to depa's official article for the current published cohort and depa announcement lists for alumni data.

footer.disclaimer: This site is operated by the Digital Economy Promotion Agency (depa), Ministry of Digital Economy and Society, Kingdom of Thailand. Alumni data is sourced from official depa announcements and is shown for informational purposes.
